description: govulncheck: 0 exploitable, 4 advisory in unused code (edwards25519@v1.1.0 GO-2026-4503; x/image@v0.35.0 GO-2026-4815, GO-2026-4961, GO-2026-4962)
prior Finding 1: cmd /c start replaced with rundll32 url.dll,FileProtocolHandler — fixed
prior Finding 2: still by-design shell exec; --no-hooks flag added (partial). Recurring as Medium with reduced impact.
prior Finding 3: maxParseDepth=100 + maxEvalDepth=100 fail-closed — fixed
prior Finding 4: now parameterized with ? placeholders — fixed
prior Finding 5: still no SHA validation, no --end-of-options on most sites; only pkg/loader/git.go:156 uses --end-of-options — recurring Medium
prior Finding 6: still concatenates "i."+field for unknown fields. Validator upstream gates production paths; SQLBuilder still self-unsafe — recurring Low (latent)
prior Finding 7: still uses backtickQuote+escapeSQLString string-escape; no regex validation as recommended — recurring Low
prior Finding 8: IN list still has no cap; unbounded values slice — recurring Low
prior Finding 9: io.Copy still has no LimitReader; mitigated by SHA256+host-pin but defense-in-depth still missing — recurring Low
prior Finding 10: error wrapped with %w still surfaces source.Path (DSN) up the stack to status bar — recurring Low
prior Finding 11: --grep= still passes pattern unescaped; extractor.go:157 correctly QuoteMeta. Inconsistent — recurring Low
prior Finding 12: 3 sites still discard errors. asOfResolved silently empty causes wrong --as-of data — recurring Low
prior Finding 13: Windows lock takeover Remove+Rename race still present; deemed acceptable — recurring Low
prior Finding 14: still hardcoded root with no password; no BT_DOLT_USER/BT_DOLT_PASSWORD env vars added — recurring Low
prior Finding 15: tea.NewProgram and p.Run() not wrapped in defer recover; terminal could be left in raw mode on panic — recurring Low

NEW: bt tail compact/human formats emit Summary and Title verbatim with %s; comments/titles containing newlines split a single event across multiple lines, breaking line-oriented downstream parsers (log injection class).
NEW: ring buffer JSONL persistence — typed Event struct, 1MiB line cap, corrupt-line tolerant, fixed path under user home — clean
NEW: runGit uses literal hardcoded git args; no user-input flow; safe
NEW: yaml.Unmarshal with typed Config and Validate(); safe
NEW: typed json.Unmarshal into string for metadata; safe
NEW: hash-based path under user config dir, 0644 perms, typed JSON; safe
NEW: --as-of and --diff-since user flags flow into git rev-parse via pkg/loader/git.go (uses --end-of-options) — safe at entry, but flagged for the correlator path which is Finding 5
NEW: pure-Go embedding/scoring, no SQL — safe
NEW: bt blindly trusts host=127.0.0.1; if Dolt config.yaml has been tampered to bind elsewhere, bt has no validation. Adversary needs filesystem write access already; minimal incremental risk

NEW: PreviewServer http.Server has no ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/IdleTimeout; Go default is no timeout. Local 127.0.0.1 only, but slowloris-class hang possible from a buggy local client

NEW: workflow_dispatch input fuzz_time interpolated as github.event.inputs.fuzz_time into shell run-blocks (and via step output). Mitigated by workflow_dispatch requiring repo write access. release-notes.yml uses correct env-var pattern.
BT_DEBUG/slog.Debug surfaces: BQL parser logs token kind+position only (no secrets); DSN-leak-via-error already covered as iter 10. Otherwise clean.
No AKIA/ghp_/sk_live_/sk_test_ patterns; no embedded credentials in source — clean

getCommitURL constructs URL from git remote (repo-controlled) + SHA; passes argv to rundll32/open/xdg-open (no shell metachar interpretation); URL handlers don't shell-execute path content — safe in practice
Hook env var expansion uses os.Expand (template, not shell eval); values go to cmd.Env not into the command string itself — safe (the by-design RCE via hook command itself is iter 2)
